Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Herbie: Automatically improve imprecise floating point formulas 141 upvotes, 18 comments
  2. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 169 upvotes, 23 comments
  3. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 173 upvotes, 30 comments
  4. iNaturalist 451 upvotes, 109 comments
  5. What changes when you turn a Linux box into a router 184 upvotes, 45 comments
  6. Fake Fans 123 upvotes, 29 comments
  7. Apple: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation 104 upvotes, 26 comments
  8. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 822 upvotes, 287 comments
  9. Some Unusual Trees 59 upvotes, 15 comments
  10. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 331 upvotes, 123 comments
  11. Run Linux containers on Android, no root required 150 upvotes, 55 comments
  12. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 414 upvotes, 206 comments
  13. How to make a sliding, self-locking, and predator-proof chicken coop door (2020) 111 upvotes, 47 comments
  14. The most-disliked people in the publishing industry 16 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. The house is a work of art: Frank Lloyd Wright 89 upvotes, 40 comments
  16. Delve removed from Y Combinator 361 upvotes, 221 comments
  17. The CMS is dead. Long live the CMS 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 797 upvotes, 616 comments
  19. Gold overtakes U.S. Treasuries as the largest foreign reserve asset 218 upvotes, 165 comments
  20. Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years 75 upvotes, 50 comments
  21. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 120 upvotes, 94 comments
  22. Sequential Optimal Packing for PCB Placement 13 upvotes, 5 comments
  23. The Cathedral, the Bazaar, and the Winchester Mystery House 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. Scientists are working on "everything vaccines" 40 upvotes, 34 comments
  25. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 99 upvotes, 110 comments
  26. Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor 110 upvotes, 134 comments
  27. Tesla Is Sitting on a Record 50k Unsold EVs 37 upvotes, 38 comments
  28. Why are we still using Markdown? 146 upvotes, 215 comments
  29. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 513 upvotes, 1147 comments
  30. Mbodi AI (YC P25) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 8:30pm, 4 April 2026. Source here.
